plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs and remove below-stop positions.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable broad market exposure and income.
Add a significant cash buffer of 15% for capital preservation.
This plan prioritizes safety and reduces overall portfolio risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for approximately 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. However, it will likely underperform in strong bull markets.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs and remove below-stop positions.
Add to CRWD, a strong performer, to 15% of the book.
Initiate a new position in TSLA at 5% based on its strong scanner setup.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
This plan balances growth potential with risk reduction.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing new high-quality setups. Expected drawdowns are moderate, balancing upside potential with downside protection.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all below-stop positions.
Initiate new positions in TSLA and NVDU at 10% each, based on their high scanner scores.
Increase CRWD to 15%, pressing into a working position.
Maintain a small 5% cash buffer to maximize deployed capital.
This plan aggressively leans into high-conviction setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for approximately 4% per month, focusing on stretch growth. It concentrates in high-scoring setups and uses leverage, which amplifies both potential gains and losses. This mode carries significantly higher drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs can experience decay.

moves.note: Maintain cash for stability and yield

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs and below-stop positions.
Reduce SMH and CRWD exposure to 5% each, cutting high-beta single names.
Increase QQQI to 30% for a stronger income focus and monthly distributions.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer for stability and potential yield opportunities.
This plan prioritizes steady income and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It significantly reduces high-beta exposure and leverages income-generating ETFs. While aiming for steady distributions and low drawdown, NAV can still fall in selloffs, and distributions are not guaranteed.
